message:
fix(pager): keep db_size strictly monotonic across cross-process peers
to eliminate BusySnapshot "page N > snapshot db_size" errors (#70)

When two processes share a WAL-mode database and extend the file
concurrently, our pager could momentarily publish a db_size *smaller*
than what a peer had already committed. That window let readers see
`page N > snapshot db_size (N-1)` and fault with `BusySnapshot`, and it
let the flusher's post-commit bookkeeping regress `inner.db_size` below
a peer's just-committed extent. This commit closes every remaining
site where we could regress db_size, and concentrates the one
legitimate shrink path (truncate-checkpoint) so it stays explicit.

Sites hardened to `fetch_max` / `.max()` semantics:

1. `PagerInner::refresh_committed_state` (~L1585) — after parsing a
   page-1 header from WAL, overwriting `self.db_size` with the header
   value could shrink it below a larger size another connection (or
   our own flusher) has already committed. Switched to
   `self.db_size = self.db_size.max(db_size)` and derive `next_page`
   from that clamped value so phantom reads can't appear.

2. `PublishedPagerState::publish_commit` (~L3494) — previously used
   `store` on the "newer-or-equal publication" branch and `fetch_max`
   only for older publications. Under cross-process interleaving a
   publisher whose `visible_commit_seq` advanced past the previous one
   can still carry a *stale* local db_size (e.g. a non-extending
   commit following a peer's EOF extension whose page-1 header we
   haven't observed). Always use `fetch_max` for db_size here —
   `visible_commit_seq` still advances via its own `fetch_max`, and
   the truncate-checkpoint path below is the only publisher allowed
   to shrink db_size.

3. `PublishedPagerState::publish_truncate_checkpoint` (~L3294) — this
   is the sole legitimate shrink path. It now re-stores the
   authoritative `update.db_size` with `Release` semantics *after*
   `finalize_publish` returns (whose internal `publish_commit` is
   now monotonic-only). Commented to make clear this is safe because
   the checkpoint lock prevents a concurrent writer from clobbering
   the store with a stale larger value. Kept `publish_snapshot_commit`
   (~L3548) aligned with the same invariant (fetch_max only; the
   comment now points at #70).

4. `PagerInner::commit` WAL Phase C1 (~L7959) and
   `commit_group_participant` mirror-path (~L8306) — the old guard
   `if self.journal_mode != JournalMode::Wal` meant WAL-mode commits
   trusted the flusher's `final_db_size` and skipped the store
   entirely, but the flusher's view can lag the real max committed
   page when peers extend the same file. Replaced both sites with
   `inner.db_size = inner.db_size.max(committed_db_size)` so the
   consolidated db_size never regresses below what we just committed
   and so peer extensions observed in subsequent `refresh_committed_\
   state` calls remain monotonically visible.

Net effect: `BusySnapshot` noise from cross-process WAL writers
disappears, truncate-checkpoint still correctly shrinks the file,
and `visible_commit_seq` / `db_size` are now consistently "strictly
monotonic except where the checkpoint lock explicitly says otherwise."
